== * 3122005000869 == Commons category (P373) == * Wakae-Kagami shrine == street address (P6375) == * {'text': '大阪府東大阪市若江南町2丁目3-9', 'language': 'ja'} == image of entrance (P9721) == * Wakaekagami-jinja Torii1.jpg {{commons category|Category:Wakae-Kagami shrine}} [[JA:若江鏡神社]] {{wikidata link|Q28693184}} == Japanese content == {{神社 |名称 = Wakae Kagami Shrine |画像 = [[File:Kagamij2.jpg|270px]]
Haiden |所在地 = {{ill|lt=Osaka Prefecture|Q122723|WD=Q122723|1=Osaka Prefecture}}{{ill|lt=Higashiōsaka|Q243863|WD=Q243863|1=Higashiōsaka}}Wakaeminami-chō 2-3-9 |位置 = {{WikidataCoord|display=inline,title}} |祭神 = Ōikazuchi Ōkami, {{ill|lt=Emperor Chūai|Q179971|WD=Q179971|1=Emperor Chūai}}, {{ill|lt=Empress Jingū|Q232803|WD=Q232803|1=Empress Jingū}} |社格 = {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Shikinaisha|1=Shikinaisha|WD=Q134917286}} (small) / former {{ill|lt=Modern shrine ranking system#other shrines|Q712534|WD=Q712534|lt=gōsha|1=Modern system of ranked Shinto shrines}} |創建 = Unknown |本殿 = Three-bay {{ill|lt=nagare-zukuri|Q210684|WD=Q210684|1=Nagare-zukuri}} |別名 = |札所等 = |例祭 = October 10 |神事 = |地図 = {{Location map|Japan Osaka|label=Wakae Kagami Shrine|position=bottom|width=200|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=15|float=center|caption=}} {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=12|marker=religious-shinto|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|frame-height=200}} }} '''Wakae Kagami Shrine''' (わかえかがみじんじゃ) is a {{ill|lt=Shinto shrine|Q845945|WD=Q845945|1=Shinto shrine}} in Wakaeminami-chō, {{ill|lt=Higashiōsaka|Q243863|WD=Q243863|1=Higashiōsaka}}, {{ill|lt=Osaka Prefecture|Q122723|WD=Q122723|1=Osaka Prefecture}}. It is a {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Shikinaisha|1=Shikinaisha|WD=Q134917286}}, and its former {{ill|lt=shrine rank|Q10444029|WD=Q10444029|1=Shakaku}} was {{ill|lt=gōsha|Q134917316|WD=Q134917316|1=Gō-sha}}. === Enshrined deities === * Main deities - Ōikazuchi Ōkami, {{ill|lt=Emperor Chūai|Q179971|WD=Q179971|1=Emperor Chūai}} * Associated hall deity - {{ill|lt=Empress Jingū|Q232803|WD=Q232803|1=Empress Jingū}} === History === Its founding year is unknown. In the section for the fourth month of {{ill|lt=Saikō|Q1324664|WD=Q1324664|1=Saikō}} 1 ({{ill|lt=854|Q24981|WD=Q24981|1=854}}) in the '{{ill|lt=Nihon Montoku Tennō Jitsuroku|Q1052905|WD=Q1052905|1=Nihon Montoku Tennō Jitsuroku}}', there is a record of advancing divine rank to "Junior Fifth Rank Lower for Ōikazuchi-Hoake-no-Myojin of {{ill|lt=Kawachi Province|Q1047150|WD=Q1047150|1=Kawachi Province}}," thought to refer to Wakae Kagami Shrine. This shrine is also listed in the '{{ill|lt=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|Q11064932|WD=Q11064932|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō}}', compiled in {{ill|lt=Enchō (era)|Q1327274|WD=Q1327274|lt=Enchō|1=Enchō}} 5 ({{ill|lt=927|Q30470|WD=Q30470|1=927}}). Because {{ill|lt=Wakae Castle|Q11616583|WD=Q11616583|1=Wakae Castle}} stood north of the enshrined site, it was revered by successive castle lords from {{ill|lt=Hatakeyama Masanaga|Q5680891|WD=Q5680891|1=Hatakeyama Masanaga}} to {{ill|lt=Miyoshi Yoshitsugu|Q986767|WD=Q986767|1=Miyoshi Yoshitsugu}}. During the {{ill|lt=Summer Siege of Osaka|Q475780|WD=Q475780|lt=Osaka Campaign|1=Siege of Osaka}} in {{ill|lt=Keichō|Q1328185|WD=Q1328185|1=Keichō}} 20 ({{ill|lt=1615|Q6784|WD=Q6784|1=1615}}), it was struck by military fire in the {{ill|lt=Battle of Yao and Wakae|Q10892144|WD=Q10892144|lt=Battle of Wakae|1=Battle of Yao (1615)}} on May 6, and shrine buildings and treasures burned. In {{ill|lt=Bunsei|Q1327119|WD=Q1327119|1=Bunsei}} 11 ({{ill|lt=1828|Q7570|WD=Q7570|1=1828}}) of the {{ill|lt=Edo period|Q184963|WD=Q184963|1=Edo period}}, the honden was rebuilt. In {{ill|lt=1872|Q7753|WD=Q7753|1=1872}} ({{ill|lt=Meiji|Q3125096|WD=Q3125096|1=Meiji era}} 5) it was ranked {{ill|lt=gōsha|Q134917316|WD=Q134917316|1=Gō-sha}}, and in January {{ill|lt=1908|Q2056|WD=Q2056|1=1908}} (Meiji 41) it was designated a shrine receiving offerings of sacred food and silk. === Precinct === * Honden (Higashiōsaka City designated {{ill|lt=tangible cultural property|Q2901860|WD=Q2901860|1=Tangible Cultural Property (Japan)}}) - rebuilt in {{ill|lt=Bunsei|Q1327119|WD=Q1327119|1=Bunsei}} 11 ({{ill|lt=1828|Q7570|WD=Q7570|1=1828}}). Valuable as shrine architecture from the {{ill|lt=Bunka-Bunsei period|Q11435433|WD=Q11435433|lt=Bunka-Bunsei era|1=Ōgosho period}}. It has an unusual structure with an associated hall inserted between two ikken-sha {{ill|lt=nagare-zukuri|Q210684|WD=Q210684|1=Nagare-zukuri}} halls. * Haiden * Shrine office * Kagami-zuka - said to be where {{ill|lt=Empress Jingū|Q232803|WD=Q232803|1=Empress Jingū}} stopped on her return from {{ill|lt=Conquest of the Three Han|Q11249435|WD=Q11249435|1=Three Han Campaign}} and buried a mirror. * Distant worship site === Sessha and massha === * {{ill|lt=Ise Jingū|Q687168|WD=Q687168|lt=Amaterasu Kōdaijin Shrine|1=Ise Grand Shrine}} * Tsukamoto {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Inari Shrine|1=UNKNOWN}} * {{ill|lt=Kumano Gongen|Q11568925|WD=Q11568925|1=Kumano Gongen}} Shrine * Suijin Shrine * Yakumo {{ill|lt=Kangiten|Q11545315|WD=Q11545315|1=Kangiten}} Shrine * Goen Jizō Shrine - enshrines Jizō-son. Remnant of {{ill|lt=shinbutsu shūgō|Q2279088|WD=Q2279088|1=Shinbutsu-shūgō}}. === Cultural properties === === Higashiōsaka City designated tangible cultural properties === * Wakae Kagami Shrine honden, with two canopies - designated March 25, {{ill|lt=1974|Q2478|WD=Q2478|1=1974}} ({{ill|lt=Shōwa|Q327567|WD=Q327567|1=Shōwa era}} 49). * {{ill|lt=Daihannya Haramita-kyō|Q15905785|WD=Q15905785|lt=Daihannya sutra in wooden box|1=Large Prajñāpāramitā Sūtras}}, 600 volumes - dedicated in {{ill|lt=Genroku|Q1325001|WD=Q1325001|1=Genroku}} 11 ({{ill|lt=1699|Q7710|WD=Q7710|1=1699}}) for rain prayer. === Festivals === * Grand annual festival, October 10 === Bibliography === * Shrine bulletin board * {{Cite book |和書 |author= Yoshinori Kunii|year= 1993|title= Travel of Ancient Shrines: Settsu, Kawachi, Izumi|publisher= Izumi Shoin|page= |id= |isbn= 4870886154|quote= }} === Notes === {{Reflist}} === External links === {{Commonscat|Wakae-Kagami shrine|Wakae Kagami Shrine}} * [http://wakaekagami.jp/ Wakae Kagami Shrine] - official website ** {{Facebook|wakae.kagami}} == Categories == [[Category:Wikidata generated shikinaisha pages|Wakae Kagami Shrine]][[Category:Shikinaisha|Wakae Kagami Shrine]][[Category:Shikinai Shōsha]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Kawachi Province]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Osaka Prefecture]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Kawachi Province]] [[Category:Former Gō-sha in Osaka Prefecture]] [[Category:東大阪市の歴史]] [[Category:Buildings in Higashiosaka, Osaka]] [[category:若江]]

revision.comment: Text replacement - "Buildings in " to "Buildings and structures in "

[[commons:Category:Wakae-Kagami shrine]][[ja:若江鏡神社]] {{Expand Japanese|若江鏡神社}} {{Infobox religious building | name = Wakae Kagami Shrine | native_name = 若江鏡神社 | image = Kagamij2.jpg | mapframe = yes | mapframe-zoom = 15 | mapframe-wikidata = yes | mapframe-point = none | map_type = Japan | map_caption = Wakae Kagami Shrine | coordinates = {{coord|34.652878|135.603823}} | map_relief = 1 | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] | website = {{Official website|http://wakaekagami.jp/}} }} {{nihongo|'''Wakae Kagami Shrine'''|若江鏡神社}} is a [[shinto shrine]] in the [[Engishiki Jinmyōchō]]. Wakaekagami-jinja Torii1.jpg {{commons category|Category:Wakae-Kagami shrine}} [[JA:若江鏡神社]] {{wikidata link|Q28693184}} == Japanese content == {{神社 |名称 = Wakae Kagami Shrine |画像 = [[File:Kagamij2.jpg|270px]]
Haiden |所在地 = {{ill|lt=Osaka Prefecture|Q122723|WD=Q122723|1=Osaka Prefecture}}{{ill|lt=Higashiōsaka|Q243863|WD=Q243863|1=Higashiōsaka}}Wakaeminami-chō 2-3-9 |位置 = {{WikidataCoord|display=inline,title}} |祭神 = Ōikazuchi Ōkami, {{ill|lt=Emperor Chūai|Q179971|WD=Q179971|1=Emperor Chūai}}, {{ill|lt=Empress Jingū|Q232803|WD=Q232803|1=Empress Jingū}} |社格 = {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Shikinaisha|1=Shikinaisha|WD=Q134917286}} (small) / former {{ill|lt=Modern shrine ranking system#other shrines|Q712534|WD=Q712534|lt=gōsha|1=Modern system of ranked Shinto shrines}} |創建 = Unknown |本殿 = Three-bay {{ill|lt=nagare-zukuri|Q210684|WD=Q210684|1=Nagare-zukuri}} |別名 = |札所等 = |例祭 = October 10 |神事 = |地図 = {{Location map|Japan Osaka|label=Wakae Kagami Shrine|position=bottom|width=200|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=15|float=center|caption=}} {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=12|marker=religious-shinto|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|frame-height=200}} }} '''Wakae Kagami Shrine''' (わかえかがみじんじゃ) is a {{ill|lt=Shinto shrine|Q845945|WD=Q845945|1=Shinto shrine}} in Wakaeminami-chō, {{ill|lt=Higashiōsaka|Q243863|WD=Q243863|1=Higashiōsaka}}, {{ill|lt=Osaka Prefecture|Q122723|WD=Q122723|1=Osaka Prefecture}}. It is a {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Shikinaisha|1=Shikinaisha|WD=Q134917286}}, and its former {{ill|lt=shrine rank|Q10444029|WD=Q10444029|1=Shakaku}} was {{ill|lt=gōsha|Q134917316|WD=Q134917316|1=Gō-sha}}. === Enshrined deities === * Main deities - Ōikazuchi Ōkami, {{ill|lt=Emperor Chūai|Q179971|WD=Q179971|1=Emperor Chūai}} * Associated hall deity - {{ill|lt=Empress Jingū|Q232803|WD=Q232803|1=Empress Jingū}} === History === Its founding year is unknown. In the section for the fourth month of {{ill|lt=Saikō|Q1324664|WD=Q1324664|1=Saikō}} 1 ({{ill|lt=854|Q24981|WD=Q24981|1=854}}) in the '{{ill|lt=Nihon Montoku Tennō Jitsuroku|Q1052905|WD=Q1052905|1=Nihon Montoku Tennō Jitsuroku}}', there is a record of advancing divine rank to "Junior Fifth Rank Lower for Ōikazuchi-Hoake-no-Myojin of {{ill|lt=Kawachi Province|Q1047150|WD=Q1047150|1=Kawachi Province}}," thought to refer to Wakae Kagami Shrine. This shrine is also listed in the '{{ill|lt=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|Q11064932|WD=Q11064932|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō}}', compiled in {{ill|lt=Enchō (era)|Q1327274|WD=Q1327274|lt=Enchō|1=Enchō}} 5 ({{ill|lt=927|Q30470|WD=Q30470|1=927}}). Because {{ill|lt=Wakae Castle|Q11616583|WD=Q11616583|1=Wakae Castle}} stood north of the enshrined site, it was revered by successive castle lords from {{ill|lt=Hatakeyama Masanaga|Q5680891|WD=Q5680891|1=Hatakeyama Masanaga}} to {{ill|lt=Miyoshi Yoshitsugu|Q986767|WD=Q986767|1=Miyoshi Yoshitsugu}}. During the {{ill|lt=Summer Siege of Osaka|Q475780|WD=Q475780|lt=Osaka Campaign|1=Siege of Osaka}} in {{ill|lt=Keichō|Q1328185|WD=Q1328185|1=Keichō}} 20 ({{ill|lt=1615|Q6784|WD=Q6784|1=1615}}), it was struck by military fire in the {{ill|lt=Battle of Yao and Wakae|Q10892144|WD=Q10892144|lt=Battle of Wakae|1=Battle of Yao (1615)}} on May 6, and shrine buildings and treasures burned. In {{ill|lt=Bunsei|Q1327119|WD=Q1327119|1=Bunsei}} 11 ({{ill|lt=1828|Q7570|WD=Q7570|1=1828}}) of the {{ill|lt=Edo period|Q184963|WD=Q184963|1=Edo period}}, the honden was rebuilt. In {{ill|lt=1872|Q7753|WD=Q7753|1=1872}} ({{ill|lt=Meiji|Q3125096|WD=Q3125096|1=Meiji era}} 5) it was ranked {{ill|lt=gōsha|Q134917316|WD=Q134917316|1=Gō-sha}}, and in January {{ill|lt=1908|Q2056|WD=Q2056|1=1908}} (Meiji 41) it was designated a shrine receiving offerings of sacred food and silk. === Precinct === * Honden (Higashiōsaka City designated {{ill|lt=tangible cultural property|Q2901860|WD=Q2901860|1=Tangible Cultural Property (Japan)}}) - rebuilt in {{ill|lt=Bunsei|Q1327119|WD=Q1327119|1=Bunsei}} 11 ({{ill|lt=1828|Q7570|WD=Q7570|1=1828}}). Valuable as shrine architecture from the {{ill|lt=Bunka-Bunsei period|Q11435433|WD=Q11435433|lt=Bunka-Bunsei era|1=Ōgosho period}}. It has an unusual structure with an associated hall inserted between two ikken-sha {{ill|lt=nagare-zukuri|Q210684|WD=Q210684|1=Nagare-zukuri}} halls. * Haiden * Shrine office * Kagami-zuka - said to be where {{ill|lt=Empress Jingū|Q232803|WD=Q232803|1=Empress Jingū}} stopped on her return from {{ill|lt=Conquest of the Three Han|Q11249435|WD=Q11249435|1=Three Han Campaign}} and buried a mirror. * Distant worship site === Sessha and massha === * {{ill|lt=Ise Jingū|Q687168|WD=Q687168|lt=Amaterasu Kōdaijin Shrine|1=Ise Grand Shrine}} * Tsukamoto {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Inari Shrine|1=UNKNOWN}} * {{ill|lt=Kumano Gongen|Q11568925|WD=Q11568925|1=Kumano Gongen}} Shrine * Suijin Shrine * Yakumo {{ill|lt=Kangiten|Q11545315|WD=Q11545315|1=Kangiten}} Shrine * Goen Jizō Shrine - enshrines Jizō-son. Remnant of {{ill|lt=shinbutsu shūgō|Q2279088|WD=Q2279088|1=Shinbutsu-shūgō}}. === Cultural properties === === Higashiōsaka City designated tangible cultural properties === * Wakae Kagami Shrine honden, with two canopies - designated March 25, {{ill|lt=1974|Q2478|WD=Q2478|1=1974}} ({{ill|lt=Shōwa|Q327567|WD=Q327567|1=Shōwa era}} 49). * {{ill|lt=Daihannya Haramita-kyō|Q15905785|WD=Q15905785|lt=Daihannya sutra in wooden box|1=Large Prajñāpāramitā Sūtras}}, 600 volumes - dedicated in {{ill|lt=Genroku|Q1325001|WD=Q1325001|1=Genroku}} 11 ({{ill|lt=1699|Q7710|WD=Q7710|1=1699}}) for rain prayer. === Festivals === * Grand annual festival, October 10 === Bibliography === * Shrine bulletin board * {{Cite book |和書 |author= Yoshinori Kunii|year= 1993|title= Travel of Ancient Shrines: Settsu, Kawachi, Izumi|publisher= Izumi Shoin|page= |id= |isbn= 4870886154|quote= }} === Notes === {{Reflist}} === External links === {{Commonscat|Wakae-Kagami shrine|Wakae Kagami Shrine}} * [http://wakaekagami.jp/ Wakae Kagami Shrine] - official website ** {{Facebook|wakae.kagami}} == Categories == [[Category:Wikidata generated shikinaisha pages|Wakae Kagami Shrine]][[Category:Shikinaisha|Wakae Kagami Shrine]][[Category:Shikinai Shōsha]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Kawachi Province]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Osaka Prefecture]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Kawachi Province]] [[Category:Former Gō-sha in Osaka Prefecture]] [[Category:東大阪市の歴史]] [[Category:Buildings and structures in Higashiosaka, Osaka]] [[category:若江]]
